title: Bridge Essay: Whole-Brain Deactivations Precede Uninduced Mind-Blanking Reports.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Non-fabrication** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Spontaneous thought — mind-wandering, default mode network, creative cognition, involuntary thought.

**Whole-Brain Deactivations Precede Uninduced Mind-Blanking Reports.**. J Neurosci.. PMID: 37643862. DOI: 10.1523/JNEUROSCI.0696-23.2023. Mind-blanking (MB) is termed as the inability to report our immediate-past mental content. In contrast to mental states with reportable content, such as mind-wandering or sensory perceptions, the neural correlates of MB started getting elucidated only recently. Anotable particularity that pertains to MB studies is the way MB is instructed for reporting, like by deliberately asking participants to "empty their minds."
